title: clerodendrum johnstonii subsp. marsabitense
intro: Clerodendrum johnstonii subsp. marsabitense, also known as the Marsabit Glory Bower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which are thought to include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Clerodendrum johnstonii subsp. marsabitense are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
